verses.text: Isaiah writes 'Maher-shalal-hash-baz' and fathers a son so named, for before the child can speak, Damascus and Samaria shall be plundered. The name preaches swift judgment. God dates the fall of the foes by a baby's first words.

Isaiah writes 'Maher-shalal-hash-baz' and fathers a son so named, for before the child can speak, Damascus and Samaria shall be plundered. The name preaches swift judgment. God dates the fall of the foes by a baby's first words.

Isaiah writes 'Maher-shalal-hash-baz' and fathers a son so named, for before the child can speak, Damascus and Samaria shall be plundered. The name preaches swift judgment. God dates the fall of the foes by a baby's first words.

Because this people despised the gentle waters of Shiloah, the mighty river of Assyria shall flood up to the neck, 'O Immanuel.' Refused grace brings a deluge. To scorn God's quiet mercy is to invite an overwhelming scourge.

Because this people despised the gentle waters of Shiloah, the mighty river of Assyria shall flood up to the neck, 'O Immanuel.' Refused grace brings a deluge. To scorn God's quiet mercy is to invite an overwhelming scourge.

Because this people despised the gentle waters of Shiloah, the mighty river of Assyria shall flood up to the neck, 'O Immanuel.' Refused grace brings a deluge. To scorn God's quiet mercy is to invite an overwhelming scourge.
Let the peoples gather and arm; they shall be broken, 'for God is with us.' Their plots come to nothing. With Immanuel on His people's side, every counsel against them fails.
Let the peoples gather and arm; they shall be broken, 'for God is with us.' Their plots come to nothing. With Immanuel on His people's side, every counsel against them fails.

God warns Isaiah not to fear what the people fear, but to 'sanctify the LORD of hosts himself,' making Him his dread and refuge. Holy fear casts out lesser fears. To reverence God rightly frees us from the terror of men.

God warns Isaiah not to fear what the people fear, but to 'sanctify the LORD of hosts himself,' making Him his dread and refuge. Holy fear casts out lesser fears. To reverence God rightly frees us from the terror of men.

He shall be 'a sanctuary' to those who trust, but 'a stone of stumbling' to both houses of Israel, on which many fall. The same Lord saves and snares. Christ is a refuge to faith but a rock of offence to unbelief.

He shall be 'a sanctuary' to those who trust, but 'a stone of stumbling' to both houses of Israel, on which many fall. The same Lord saves and snares. Christ is a refuge to faith but a rock of offence to unbelief.

Isaiah binds up the testimony among his disciples and resolves, 'I will wait upon the LORD,' with the children God has given him as signs. He trusts in dark days. When the nation will not hear, the faithful keep and wait on God's word.

Isaiah binds up the testimony among his disciples and resolves, 'I will wait upon the LORD,' with the children God has given him as signs. He trusts in dark days. When the nation will not hear, the faithful keep and wait on God's word.

When men say, seek the mediums and wizards, Isaiah answers, 'To the law and to the testimony,' for those without God's light see only darkness and distress. Scripture is the test. Those who turn from God's word grope in gloom with no dawn.
